nodes.x: TO the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. O clap your hands, all ye people; shout unto God with the voice of triumph.
PSALM. XLVI. Christ's reign on earth. To the chief Musician, A Psalm for the sons of Korah. QO* clap your hands, all ye people; shout unto’ God with the voice of et de
For the LORD most high [is] terrible; [he is] a great King over all the earth.
He shall subdue the people under us, and the nations under our feet.
He shall choose our inheritance for us, the excellency of Jacob whom he loved. Selah.
God is gone up with a shout, the LORD with the sound of a trumpet.
trumpet (Heb. chatsotserah) from chatsar "to sound a trumpet"
3 the glory of God. And there was a great sound, as thunder. And I said, Lord, what is this sound Ὁ
Sing praises to God, sing praises: sing praises unto our King, sing praises.
For God [is] the King of all the earth: sing ye praises with understanding.
God reigneth over the heathen: God sitteth upon the throne of his holiness.
face of him that sitteth on the throne. This expression is a reference to God the Father

13. The light which is in all things, which giveth life to all
 things, which is the law by which all things are governed, even the
 power of God who sitteth upon his throne, who is in the bosom of
 eternity, who is in the midst of all things.

13 The light which is in all things, which giveth life to all things, which is the law by which all things are governed, even the power of God who sitteth upon his throne, who is in the bosom of eternity, who is in the midst of all things.
The princes of the people are gathered together, [even] the people of the God of Abraham: for the shields of the earth [belong] unto God: he is greatly exalted.
